extractedFieldValues.current_value: The patient, John Slatauski, age 85, has been referred for home health services following a recent left shoulder arthroscopy and rotator cuff repair performed on April 10, 2026, due to a complete rotator cuff tear. His primary diagnosis is the post-surgical condition related to the left shoulder surgery (ICD-10-CM M75.122), and he has multiple comorbidities, including type 2 diabetes mellitus, hypertension, and a history of vitamin D deficiency, which further complicate his recovery process. Post-operatively, the patient presents with significant functional limitations including severe left shoulder pain (rated 9/10), which restricts his ability to perform activities of daily living (ADLs) such as dressing, bathing, and mobility. He requires skilled nursing care for monitoring his surgical site, medication management, and education on disease processes and post-hospital management. Additionally, he will benefit from physical therapy to aid in rehabilitation, focusing on pain management, strengthening, and establishing a safe home exercise program to prevent falls and aid in recovery.
source_spans: Primary Reason for Home Health/Medical Necessity (POC Element):
Post-operatively, the patient presents with significant functional limitations including severe left shoulder pain (rated 9/10)
